By me and AIDo you prefer solving problems on your own, calmly and without unnecessary advice? Then Playwright may be a good fit for you. It helps you do great work without having to convince the whole team during a meeting.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

What is it?What Is Playwright?
Playwright is a web automation tool. It is used for:

• testing applications,

• automating different tasks,

• and, more and more often, powering AI agents.
With one API, you can control Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit. You can use it to write tests, create scripts, or build agent workflows.
Playwright supports TypeScript, Python, .NET, and Java, so you can choose the language that works best for you.
One of its biggest advantages is that once you understand how it works, you can use it independently. You do not need someone standing over your shoulder and explaining every step. The documentation and the tool itself are designed to be clear and easy to use.
Three Tools That Save Time and Energy
Playwright Test is a complete test runner. It automatically waits for page elements, includes built-in assertions and tracing, and can run tests in parallel across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit. In other words, it helps you build a reliable test suite quickly.
Playwright CLI provides browser automation for coding agents such as Claude Code and GitHub Copilot. It uses skill-based workflows and does not require a large amount of context. This means less setup and more time spent doing the actual work.
Playwright MCP gives AI agents full control of a browser through structured accessibility snapshots. It brings us one step closer to automating routine work without constant supervision.
You Do Not Have to Write Everything by Hand
Playwright lets you write tests directly in code. You can also record actions in the browser and automatically generate code that you can edit later.
There is no reason to feel bad about using generated code. Playwright usually produces cleaner and shorter code than competing tools.
A good approach is to click through the process first and then adjust the details in the generated code.
Trace Viewer: See What Happened
Playwright also includes Trace Viewer, a visual tool for reviewing recorded test traces.
You can move forward and backward through the timeline and see what happened during every test action. When something fails, you can investigate it yourself, step by step.
Free, Open Source, and No Commitments
Playwright is available on GitHub and is completely free.
This means small companies and individual developers do not need to pay for expensive tools such as Tosca. Playwright can also be a more modern and powerful option for many browser automation tasks than older tools such as Selenium or JMeter.
Playwright also has an official extension for VS Code. You can manage your testing in a familiar environment without constantly switching between applications.
Why Playwright Is Worth Using
In summary, Playwright is a tool that respects the way you work. It gives you:

• independent work from start to finish, from writing a test to analyzing an error,

• quick access to answers through clear documentation, generated code, and Trace Viewer,

• clarity and peace of mind through visual debugging tools,

• and all of this for free, without having to justify the cost of an expensive license to management.
You can learn more on the official website: playwright.dev
